summary: Metronidazole is recommended due to its safety profile in pregnancy; if not feasible or contraindicated, alternative antibiotics like clindamycin may also be considered based on effectiveness but less favorable risk-benefit ratio compared to metronidazole. The primary recommendation remains \boxed{\textbf{Metronidazole}} because of its well-established efficacy and safety in managing bacterial vaginosis safely throughout gestation without compromising fetal health outcomes significantly better than other options available clinically today under similar circumstances presented here regarding treatment choice considerations pertinent only within
